steps.title: Identify the mass relationship
steps.content: We know that $r$ identical objects have a total mass of $t$ kilograms. Since all objects are identical, the total mass is directly proportional to the number of objects — double the objects, double the mass; triple the objects, triple the mass.

steps.title: Scale the mass for 112r objects
steps.content:
We need the mass of $112r$ objects, which is exactly 112 times as many objects as the original $r$. When we multiply the quantity by 112, the total mass must also multiply by 112.
$$\text{New mass} = 112 \times t = 112t \text{ kg}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
